codeReview.summary: The ErrorBoundary component implementation is complete and correctly fulfills all requirements. The component is a class-based React error boundary using getDerivedStateFromError and componentDidCatch, providing a user-friendly fallback UI with a 'Try Again' reset button and console.error logging. The implementation includes accessibility features (roles, labels, hints), development mode error details, and custom fallback support. All 14 tests pass covering normal rendering, error catching, reset functionality, console logging, custom fallback, and accessibility.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker updated jest.config.js to configure ts-jest with JSX support (jsx: 'react-jsx'). This configuration enables TSX testing but wasn't previously documented in AGENTS.md or services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: jest.config.js lines 31-36: ts-jest configured with jsx: 'react-jsx'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker added __DEV__ global mock to jest.setup.ts. This is essential for React Native development mode detection in tests but wasn't documented previously.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: jest.setup.ts line 12: (global as Record<string, unknown>).__DEV__ = true

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker added testID prop to Button component for testability. This follows a pattern that could be documented as a convention for all interactive components.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/ui/Button.tsx: added testID?: string prop and testID={testID} on Pressable

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker added StyleSheet.flatten mock and additional React Native component mocks (View, Text, ScrollView, Pressable, ActivityIndicator, Button) to jest.setup.ts. This pattern for RN component mocking in Jest should be documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: jest.setup.ts lines 22-30: StyleSheet.flatten mock and component string mocks

summary: PASS: The ErrorBoundary component implementation fully satisfies all requirements (VAL-ERR-001, VAL-ERR-002, VAL-ERR-003). The component correctly catches JavaScript errors, displays a user-friendly fallback UI with 'Try Again' button, logs errors to console.error, and supports custom fallbacks. The 14 comprehensive tests verify all functionality including reset behavior, error re-throwing when underlying issue persists, and accessibility features. No blocking issues found. Minor shared state observations noted regarding Jest configuration enhancements for TSX and React Native testing.
